findings.summary: Generation flows mix throw/null/result error contracts across adjacent modules
evidence: The review context flags a sync/async contract mix for core generation paths spanning hooks, lib transforms, and shot editor services.
Error strategy snapshots across these modules show mixed patterns (throw, return_null, try_catch/mixed), increasing caller-side branching and ambiguity.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one boundary contract for generation orchestration (typed Result or AppError throw), then normalize adapters in the four modules so each layer exposes one consistent failure mode.
findings.reasoning: Consistent error contracts reduce defensive glue code and make failure behavior predictable during task orchestration.

findings.summary: A few shared modules carry disproportionate fan-in and mixed responsibilities
evidence: Import-graph context lists these as top dependency hubs (224, 185, 148, and 136 importers respectively).
Each file blends multiple concerns (normalization + UI toast side effects, broad type barreling, bootstrap/runtime instrumentation composition, and generic helpers), which widens blast radius.
findings.suggestion: Split these hubs by responsibility (error normalization vs presentation, type barrels by domain, bootstrap phases, utility domains) and keep thin compositional entrypoints.
findings.reasoning: Reducing high-fan-in mixed abstractions improves locality and makes incremental refactors safer.

findings.summary: Several hooks are heavily comment-scaffolded relative to codebase norms
evidence: AI debt signals show comment ratios of 0.80, 0.89, 0.62, and 0.38 in these files versus a 0.068 codebase average.
The same set includes template-like explanatory scaffolding that mirrors implementation details instead of capturing policy-level intent.
findings.suggestion: Trim restating comments, keep only policy/why comments, and extract repeated guard/logging scaffolds into named helpers where behavior repeats.
findings.reasoning: Reducing template-like scaffolding improves signal-to-noise and lowers maintenance cost when behavior evolves.
